student_name: Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari
hub_subtitle: Inglês do dia a dia — apresentar-se, rotina e conversação

lesson.menu_desc: Talking about health, energy, and wellbeing, and giving advice with should and must -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 9 -- Health & Wellbeing
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari | Lesson 9 -- Health & Wellbeing

listenings.text: A year ago, I felt exhausted every single day. My job was very stressful, I slept badly, and I had no energy at all. My doctor told me I had to make some changes for my wellbeing, so I started small. First, I decided to cut down on sugar and coffee, and I began to eat a more balanced diet. I also went for a short walk every morning to feel fit again. But the most important change was sleep. Now I get eight hours a night, and I avoid my phone before bed. I still have busy weeks, of course, but I cope with stress much better now. I take short, mindful breaks during the day, and honestly, I feel like a completely new person.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah! I just got back from my yearly checkup, and I wanted to share something with you. The doctor said I am basically healthy, but I must reduce my stress. She told me I should not work through my lunch break, and that I should take a short walk instead. She also said I must sleep more and cut down on caffeine, because it was affecting my energy. Honestly, it was a good reminder. We are both so busy, and we often ignore the small symptoms our body gives us. Let us promise to take better care of our wellbeing this year. Maybe we can go for a walk together on the weekend. Call me back. Bye!

extra_audio.text: Notice how your body feels and name the main symptom. Say what the person should do to feel better. Say what they should avoid or cut down on. Say what they must do if the problem is serious. Give one final, kind piece of advice.
